SUMMARY: The My Lib Power Pages site requires exactly two custom tables: lpulga_libraryitem (catalog entries) and lpulga_webmessage (contact form submissions). Both were set up in a prior agent run and their schemas fully match the current site code. The standard contact table is reused as-is for portal authentication and the existing 1:N contact→lpulga_webmessage relationship is preserved. No new tables or columns are required.

TABLES_DATA.rationale: Stores the catalog entries displayed on the /catalog page and in CatalogCard components. All five columns in the manifest match the Item TypeScript type used in Catalog.tsx and CatalogCard.tsx exactly: lpulga_name→title, lpulga_author→author, lpulga_excerpt→excerpt, lpulga_imgid→Unsplash photo ID, lpulga_externalid→URL-safe routing key (used by the /catalog/item/${id} link in CatalogCard). No new columns are evidenced by the current code. The Catalog page currently uses a hardcoded ITEMS array — this table is the intended live data source when Web API calls are wired in.

relationships.description: Read by anonymous and authenticated users via the /catalog page (no foreign key — standalone catalog entity)

TABLES_DATA.rationale: Captures contact form submissions from the /contact page. The three user-facing columns (lpulga_name, lpulga_email, lpulga_message) map exactly to the three controlled inputs in ContactForm.tsx. The lookup column lpulga_contactid links an authenticated portal user's Contact record to their submission — this 1:N relationship (contact→lpulga_webmessage) was established during the initial setup. ContactForm.tsx currently uses a setTimeout placeholder; when the Dataverse Web API call is implemented, it will POST to this table.

relationships.description: Many Web Messages submitted by one Contact (authenticated user scenario) via lpulga_contactid lookup

TABLES_DATA.rationale: Standard Dataverse table used by Power Pages as the portal user identity record. The /account page contains AccountForm.tsx which is a sign-in form (email + password fields only) — credentials are managed entirely by Power Pages native authentication, not stored as custom columns. The /profile page in .powerpages-site/web-pages/profile/ is the standard Power Pages profile page backed by this table (hiddenfromsitemap: true, confirming it is portal-internal). The contact table is also the parent in the 1:N contact→lpulga_webmessage relationship. No custom columns need to be added.

relationships.description: One Contact has many Web Messages (authenticated submissions) via lpulga_contactid on lpulga_webmessage

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Existing manifest is complete — no gaps found
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: Every field rendered by the current React components maps to a column that already exists in the manifest. Catalog.tsx's Item type (title, author, imgId, excerpt) maps directly to lpulga_libraryitem. ContactForm.tsx's three inputs (name, email, message) map directly to lpulga_webmessage. No columns are missing.

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Standard Contact table handles all auth/profile needs
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: AccountForm.tsx is a sign-in form only — email and password are Portal authentication concerns, not Dataverse columns. Power Pages manages portal user identity via the standard contact table natively. The .powerpages-site/web-pages/profile/ page (hiddenfromsitemap: true) is the standard portal profile page — no custom profile table or additional contact columns are needed.

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Existing contact→webmessage relationship is sound
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: The 1:N relationship (contact → lpulga_webmessage via Lpulga_ContactId lookup) established during initial setup is architecturally correct. It allows authenticated users' contact submissions to be linked to their Dataverse Contact record, enabling operators to see all messages from a given user. Anonymous submissions leave the lookup null — which is fine since lpulga_contactid is not required.

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Future: item detail routing already accommodated
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: CatalogCard.tsx links to /catalog/item/${item.id} but no App.tsx route or detail page component exists yet. When this is built, lpulga_externalid on lpulga_libraryitem is the intended URL-safe identifier (no schema change needed — the column already exists and was specifically designed for this purpose).

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Live Dataverse OData verification was skipped
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: Shell script execution (verify-dataverse-access.js / dataverse-request.js) is not available in this agent session. This model is based on .datamodel-manifest.json and datamodel-setup-summary.txt. The setup summary notes 'Final OData verification still returned HTTP 400' after creation. Run node scripts/validate-datamodel.js to confirm the tables actually exist in the pulgasandbox environment before attempting Web API integration.

RATIONALE_DATA.title: Web API table permissions still need configuring
RATIONALE_DATA.desc: For the catalog page to read from lpulga_libraryitem and the contact form to write to lpulga_webmessage via the Power Pages Web API, Table Permissions must be set in the portal: lpulga_libraryitem needs Global read for Anonymous Users; lpulga_webmessage needs Create for Authenticated Users (scoped to Contact). These are portal-layer configurations, not schema changes.

ER_DIAGRAM:
erDiagram
    CONTACT["contact (Contact)"] {
        guid   contactid      PK
        string fullname
        string emailaddress1
    }
    LPULGA_WEBMESSAGE["lpulga_webmessage (Web Message)"] {
        guid   lpulga_webmessageid  PK
        guid   lpulga_contactid     FK
        string lpulga_name
        string lpulga_email
        string lpulga_message
    }
    LPULGA_LIBRARYITEM["lpulga_libraryitem (Library Item)"] {
        guid   lpulga_libraryitemid  PK
        string lpulga_name
        string lpulga_author
        string lpulga_excerpt
        string lpulga_imgid
        string lpulga_externalid
    }
    CONTACT ||--o{ LPULGA_WEBMESSAGE : "submits"
